title: Prejob Guidance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Employment > Prejob Guidance 50 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Native American Community Women No Filter African American Community Native American Community Women ACCENT NETWORK IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support with resumes, job searches, credential evaluation, and digital literacy, along with career counseling and career planning guidance. Also offers financial wellness services, including workshops on saving, budgeting, and debt management, and one-on-one coaching. 12254 SW Garden Place Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers with a focus on Russian and Ukrainian population Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-8pm Email (971) 280-6738 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community events such as youth soccer, open gym, tutoring, community-specific workshops (topics include traditions, art, music, history, or language), leadership workshops, and clothing donation drives. Also assists in enrollment in the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) and helps individuals who are job searching, resume building, and with job application form completion. Provides information about rent assistance programs. 7535 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 972133905 Eligibility: Varies by service with a focus on African refugees and immigrant families Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Events: Varies by service Email (971) 430-6193 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AT:PROJECT ANTI-TRAFFICKING ALLIANCE OF CENTRAL OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Anti-Trafficking Project and Cannabis Workers Program provides human and labor trafficking hotline services, including safety planning, risk assessments, hygiene items, clothing, food, mental health, substance abuse treatment referrals, applying for public assistance benefits, and document replacement. In addition, the program offers assistance with GED, resume building, transportation to interviews, and referrals to local services for job training and career development. Case management and medical accompaniment services are also available. All services are confidential. Eligibility: Anti-Trafficking Project: Individuals who are experiencing or survivors of sex or labor trafficking or exploitation Youth who have experience Commercial Sexual Exploitation of Children (CSEC). Cannabis Workers Program: Individuals who have worked in the marijuana industry who have experienced wage loss, unsafe living and working conditions, violence, labor exploitation, or human trafficking. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 647-0658 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAY AREA FIRST STEP INC RESOURCE DEPARTMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in space for social activities. Offers assistance with resume building, help to quit smoking, and access to support groups for substance use issues and parenting. 155 S Empire Boulevard Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Individuals and families with minor children in substance use disorder recovery Hours: Monday/Thursday 8am-8pm, Tuesday-Wednesday/Friday 8am-5pm, Saturday 1pm-4pm, Sunday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 756-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES OREGON EMPLOYMENT AND INDEPENDENT LIVING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support to finding jobs and navigating state and local resources and systems, including Vocational Rehabilitation (VR), and the Office of Developmental Disability Services (ODDS). 475 Cottage Street NE Suite 120 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Deaf, DeafBlind, and Hard of Hearing individuals Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 751-2346 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS BEHAVORIAL HEALTH CENTER FOR FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health counseling that includes but is not limited to individual and family therapy, career counseling, academic school guidance, domestic violence (individual counseling and support groups), and new mother support. Also provides foster parent support and adult foster care support (life skills, personal and social development, conflict management, and budgeting). Intensive Outpatient Services (IOP) include medication management, individual therapy, and family/couples therapy as needed. Most services are provided virtually. 2665 Villa Creek Drive Farmers Branch, TX 75234 Eligibility: -Varies by service; most programs are for ages 3-19 and their adult caregivers -Adult Foster Care and Group Home Support: Individuals with complex health needs who are in a home-like or residential setting -IOP: Individuals transitioning back to work or school or who need extra support beyond an outpatient appointment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (469) 343-4081 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS CENTER OF CULTIVATING PARENTS PORTLAND BEHAVORIAL HEALTH CENTER FOR FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health counseling that includes but is not limited to individual and family therapy, career counseling, academic school guidance, domestic violence (individual counseling and support groups), and new mother support. Also provides foster parent support and adult foster care support (life skills, personal and social development, conflict management, and budgeting). Intensive Outpatient Services (IOP) include medication management, individual therapy, and family/couples therapy as needed. Most services are provided virtually. 1340 SW Bertha Boulevard Suite 201 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: -Varies by service; most programs are for ages 3-19 and their adult caregivers -Adult Foster Care and Group Home Support: Individuals with complex health needs who are in a home-like or residential setting -IOP: Individuals transitioning back to work or school or who need extra support beyond an outpatient appointment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 894-7026 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE 2ND CHANCE SHELTER PEER SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services such as limited assistance obtaining Oregon ID, mailing addresses, GED tutoring, and essential supplies as available (such as toothpaste, shampoo, soap, and deodorant). Also provides job search assistance such as preparing a resume, completing job applications, and interview techniques. Other services include: counseling including anger management, detox and rehab referrals, Oregon Health Plan enrollment assistance, and computer and internet access. 1100 Jackson Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm (541) 967-8545 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY OUTREACH THROUGH RADICAL EMPOWERMENT ZEPHYR HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a space focused on building community and life skills for young adults. Offers a variety of workshops that are suggested by participants such as sewing, harm reduction education, resume building, and more. Eligibility: Ages 16-24 Hours: Varies by program Email (541) 870-0036 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONNECTED LANE COUNTY SPARK AT BOOTH KELLY DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a youth drop-in center. Services include job finding assistance, resume and cover letter support, interview practice, career mentorship, assistance with completing college and scholarship applications, information and referral to housing and healthcare community resources, and access to shower and laundry facilities. 303 S 5th Street Suite 150 Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 Hours: Wednesday-Thursday 3pm-6pm, Friday varies by month Email (541) 654-4095 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONSTRUCTING HOPE PRE-APPRENTICESHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 10 weeks of construction training including classroom instruction, hands-on experience, certifications, and site visits to apprenticeship training centers. 405 NE Church Street Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following qualifications: -Age 18 and older -US citizen or legal resident -High school diploma or GED with reading and math skills at an 8th-grade level -Must have or be able to get a state driver’s license and auto insurance -Pass drug screening Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-noon and 1pm-4pm, Friday by appointment only Quarterly sessions each year begin in January, April, June, and September Email (503) 281-1234 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DRESS FOR SUCCESS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides work clothing and employment support services. Work-Ready Clothing: Interview and work-ready clothing to help individuals transition into the workforce. Clients receive five pieces of work-ready clothing plus shoes, a coat, and accessories when they have a job interview, and five additional pieces of clothing when they get a job. Job Preparation: Provides job preparation classes for job seekers called Fast Track. Classes are currently online only. The regular cycle of four classes repeats monthly January-November. Additional services are available once Fast Track is completed. Employment Retention: The Professional Women's Group (PWG) is an ongoing group providing the opportunity for individuals to virtually meet with working women. Includes presentations and workshops to support life and career. The PWG meets regularly for programming with speakers from the community. Examples of program themes include financial education and planning, career development, the written and unwritten rules of the workplace, health and wellness, communication, and leadership skills. 1532 NE 37th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Women and gender expansive individuals referred by a member agency Hours: Services: By appointment Job preparation: Thursday 10am-11:30am Email (503) 249-7300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DRESS FOR SUCCESS OREGON JOB SEARCH ESSENTIALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides job preparation classes teaching job search tools and strategies, resumes, cover letters, networking, and interviewing. Virtual and in-person coaching. Online and in-person classes offered in collaboration with Oregon WorkSource. 1532 NE 37th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Women and gender-expansive individuals that are actively applying for jobs and preparing for interviews Hours: Resume, Cover Letter Review Career Coach: Wednesday 10am-noon Drop-In Virtual Career Coaching: Tuesday 10am-11am, Thursday 2pm-2:30pm Virtual Interview Coaching: Tuesday-Thursday by appointment. Coffee, Donuts, and Job Searching Workshop: Thursday 10am-noon Job Prep Coaching: Wednesday 10am-11:30am Job Search Support: Thursday 10am Email (503) 249-7300 111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOODWILL JOB CONNECTION CAREER CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides training classes in Microsoft Office. Instructors offer Microsoft Office curriculum and other learning resources to help all community members reach their career goals. -Microsoft Office classes and certificates -Computer skills classes and certificates (Computer Navigation, Email Navigation, Internet Navigation) -Workplace Preparedness classes and certificates -National Career Readiness NCRC classes and certificates -GED preparation support 1943 SE Sixth Avenue Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by location (503) 238-6100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GUIDANCE 2 GREATNESS BEYOND REENTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with criminal record and eviction expungement, job development, workplace training, financial literacy, felony rights, and reentry training. May be able to provide program participants with supplies such as personal protective equipment (PPE) and a desktop computer. Eligibility: -Age 18 and older -Reentry Training for women ages 16-24 -Focus on the African American and BIPOC community Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 575-7612 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMEPLATE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 12685 SW 4th Street Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. Hours: Monday/Wednesday 3pm-5pm and 6pm-8pm, Tuesday 2pm-5pm Email (503) 608-7252 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMEPLATE HOMEPLATE THURSDAY NIGHT DROP IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 494 E Main Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. Hours: Thursday 6pm-8pm Email (503) 608-7252 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY FAMILY COALITION RESUME AND JOB SEARCH ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides guidance on resume building and job search tips. 535 E River Street Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9:00am-4:30pm Email (541) 592-6139 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION PRESCHOOL FOR ALL EARLY CHILDHOOD PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports individuals wanting to start a career in Early Childhood Education by providing career coaching (one-on-one coaching and planning, resource guidance, and referrals), assistance with building soft skills (problem-solving, organization, communication), and support services that may include transportation, textbooks, work clothes, licensing fees, and test prep. Also provides tuition assistance with 1-year certificate programs or 2-year Associate of Applied Science degrees in Early Childhood Education. The program does not assist with the creation of new in-home childcare businesses. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: -Age 16 and older -Must be authorized to work in the United States -Interested in working in Early Childhood Education jobs in Multnomah County Hours: Appointment required Email (971) 997-8261 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY LIFE SKILLS AND ONE-ON-ONE MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Classroom and mentoring program teaching budgeting, life skills, job preparation, parenting skills, and Bible study. Resource incentives may be provided for those successfully participating in the program. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 650-0153 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF TIGARD, TUALATIN, AND SHERWOOD GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, hygiene supplies, household goods, bedding/linens, over-the counter medicines and supplies, financial training, assistance with resume writing, and nutrition courses. Requests are screened and referred to outside agencies for fulfillment. Assistance is dependent upon available funding and not guaranteed. Also accepts donations of bedding/linens and personal grooming supplies. 10900 SW 121st Avenue Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Wednesday 9:30am-12:30pm Email (503) 443-6888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY LIBRARY EVENTS AND CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an online community calendar to help community members find classes and events held at local libraries. Events and classes include job seeker resources (support groups and resume workshops), computer help classes and access to computers, arts and crafts instruction, book clubs and discussion groups, chess clubs, cultural celebrations and festivals, early reading skills, teen programming, lectures and author talks, money and finances programming, performances and exhibitions, story times, and special programs for parents with children ages 0-6. Eligibility: Some classes or computer lab services may require a library card Hours: Varies by class or event Email (503) 988-5123 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EDUCATIONAL YOUTH ADVOCACY AND ENGAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an advocacy program that helps students and their families set and achieve goals in four key areas: Mind, Body, Spirit, and Context. Services include wrap-around services, advocacy services, college visits, life skills workshops, job training, financial aid assistance, school tutoring, after-school homework help, and the development of cultural pride and relevant skills. Incentives like stipends, meals, clothing, hygiene items, school supplies, and financial assistance for youth participants. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: -Portland Public School District: K-12th grade self-identified Native American youth -Successful Families 2020: K-12th grade self-identified Native American youth enrolled in select schools within David Douglas and Reynolds school districts. -SUN Youth Advocacy: 5th-10th grade self-identified Native American youth enrolled in select schools within Centennial, David Douglas, Gresham-Barlow, Parkrose, Portland Public, and Reynolds school districts. OR Region 3: 5th-10th grade any race/ethnicity enrolled in the following schools: Alice Ott Middle, Cherry Park Elementary, Gilbert Heights Elementary, Gilbert Park Elementary, Parkrose High, Parkrose Middle, Prescott Elementary, Sacramento Elementary, and Shaver Elementary Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm, some evening hours for events Email (503) 288-8177 329 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST YOUTH CORPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers residential and nonresidential job training and employment with a focus on conservation. Programs include local community crews, an internship program for college aged participants, a fully accredited alternative high school, and a two-acre organic farm. A stipend and potential high school credit can be earned while working on reforestation and recreation projects. Transportation available. 2621 Augusta Street Eugene, OR 97403 Eligibility: Youth Corps Community-based Program: Ages 15-18 Youth Corps Camping Program: Ages 16-18 Back Country Leadership Program: Ages 16-19 Community Wildfire Protection Program: Ages 19-26 Leadership Development Program: Age 19-26 Young Adult Program: Age 19 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 349-5055 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHRA OHRA COMMUNITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing services, case management, information and referral, mailing address, telephone, benefits and services assistance, job search-related printing, resume creation, hygiene supplies, and education opportunities. Showers and laundry are available twice a week. Each individual who showers can do a small load of laundry. 2350 Ashland Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers and Laundry: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm. First come, first serve. Email (541) 631-2235 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF MULTNOMAH COUNTY EAST JOBS OFFICE, CHILD WELFARE, SELF-SUFFICIENCY, FAMILY STABILITY AND EMPLOYMENT, AND TEEN PARENT SELF-SUFFICIENCY JOBS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Steps to Success, a partnership between ODHS and Portland Community College, is an integrated program that provides individuals with the tools to become self-sufficient. This program provides intensive assistance, job search skills and training, assistance to receive a GED or ABE, counseling services, alcohol/drug programs, and employment services. JOBS is the state's employment and training program for people receiving public assistance. One segment of the JOBS program, known as JOBS Plus, places TANF recipients in limited-duration training positions with private employers. 11826 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, closed major holidays (971) 673-0909 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF MULTNOMAH COUNTY GRESHAM SELF-SUFFICIENCY JOBS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF JOBS is the state's employment and training program for people receiving public assistance. One segment of the JOBS program, known as JOBS Plus, places TANF recipients in limited-duration training positions with private employers. 635 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 102 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, closed major holidays (503) 673-8500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION OF JACKSON AND JOSEPHINE COUNTY NATIONAL FARMWORKER JOBS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment and training services to assist farmworkers (seasonal or migrant) in obtaining permanent and higher-paying employment opportunities. English as a second language, GED/High school equivalency test instruction, career services, classes, training, and on-the-job experiences, skills training and certifications for farmworkers, support with the job application and search process, career counseling, financial assistance for college, and education and career services for agricultural workers and their dependents. Depending on location and funding availability, specific classes, training opportunities, and financial assistance will vary. 820 S Central Avenue Suite A Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are an agricultural worker, the dependent, or spouse of an agricultural worker who meet federal income guidelines and are authorized to in the United States. Must be enrolled or in the process of enrolling at an accredited community college, vocational school/program, apprenticeship, or 4-year university. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 881-1491 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON TRADESWOMEN TRADES AND APPRENTICESHIP CAREER CLASS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 7-week, state-certified, pre-apprenticeship training class in the skilled construction trades that provides students with the following: -Learn basic trades, math, and measurement -Learn to use hand and power tools -Receive an introduction to green building -Explore topics such as job site safety and construction culture -Gain 30 hours of hands-on experience working alongside skilled female instructors on actual job sites -Field trips to apprenticeship training centers and active construction sites -Improve physical fitness with strength training taught by a certified fitness trainer Career counselors assist program graduates with job searches and application processes to apprenticeship training programs upon graduation. Career counselors also provide individualized employment counseling throughout the entire course of the graduate's trades career. 16790 NE Mason Street Suite 100 Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: -Women and other gender minorities -Age 18 years or older -GED or high school diploma -Ability to pass a drug test -Attend an information session -Take the National Career Readiness Certificate (NCRC) test Additional enrollment requirements may be required and are available during the initial informational session Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (503) 335-8200 121 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR JUST FUTURE EMPLOYMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Employment program assists with job coaching and job placement, resume building, training, and educational costs. PATHWAYS TO SUCCESS: Helps low-income community members obtain living wage employment. LIVING SOLUTIONS: This program helps homeless and very low-income people who want to work but are not well suited for traditional employment programs. Living Solutions offers job training and job placement assistance with the goal of placing participants in living wage jobs. Services include basic education, skills training, and follow-up job coaching. COMMUNITY WORKS: Community Works is a consortium which provides a multi-cultural and individualized approach to helping low income parents to successfully transition off TANF and back into the workforce. DEPARTMENT OF COMMUNITY JUSTICE WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM: This program provides services to individuals who have been identified by the court system. Partners work together to ensure participants have the range of career coaching, training, housing and alcohol and drug treatment services they need. 10550 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 548-0200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR JUST FUTURE OUR JUST FUTURE ROCKWOOD BUILDING EMPLOYMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Employment program assists with job coaching and job placement, resume building, training, and educational costs. PATHWAYS TO SUCCESS: Helps low-income community members obtain living wage employment. LIVING SOLUTIONS: This program helps homeless and very low-income people who want to work but are not well suited for traditional employment programs. Living Solutions offers job training and job placement assistance with the goal of placing participants in living wage jobs. Services include basic education, skills training, and follow-up job coaching. COMMUNITY WORKS: Community Works is a consortium which provides a multi-cultural and individualized approach to helping low income parents to successfully transition off TANF and back into the workforce.
